focus: Copilot dispatch authority and non-mutating status readiness
keyFindings: Q-P0 exact insertion point is the cli-copilot branch in spec_kit_deep-research_auto.yaml, which reads the rendered prompt, passes it through resolveCopilotPromptArg, then launches copilot -p with --allow-all-tools and --no-ask-user.
resolveCopilotPromptArg has both raw-prompt and @path wrapper modes, so any authority preamble must survive both branches rather than only mutating the prompt file body.

Q-P2 evidence favors adding a non-mutating status readiness-action probe before lowering DEFAULT_DEBOUNCE_MS, because watcher tests already cover rapid saves, default-window coalescing, rename/delete cleanup, burst renames, concurrent renames, and symlink alias dedup.

code_graph_status currently calls getGraphFreshness and then emits readiness.action none, which hides selective_reindex/full_scan actions that detectState/ensureCodeGraphReady can already distinguish.
